harun_in_the_book.title: Harun, prophet granted by mercy
harun_in_the_book.introduction: Harun (هارون عليه السلام) is the brother of the prophet Musa. Allah mentions him in Surah Maryam, right after recalling Musa's prophethood: "And out of Our grace We granted him his brother Aaron as a prophet." (19:53) This verse establishes Harun's nubuwwa (prophethood) as a gift of divine rahma (mercy), without detailing his entire mission here.
harun_in_the_book.conclusion: Harun is thus recognized as a nabi (prophet) in the Quran. Allah granted him to Musa through mercy, a sign that prophetic guidance can be shared through brotherhood and mutual support.
harun_in_the_book.moral_lesson: Allah grants prophethood to whom He wills. Harun teaches us that divine mercy can unite brothers in a shared mission of truth.
harun_in_the_book.historical_context: The Quran mentions Harun in Musa's account without developing his full biography here. The believer adheres to the revealed text.

chapters.title: A prophet given to Musa
chapters.content: Allah says: "And out of Our grace We granted him his brother Aaron as a prophet." (19:53) The preceding verse recalls that Musa was called and brought near to Allah. Then Allah announces that He granted Harun as a nabi. This gift is described as rahma: Harun's prophethood is a favor, not only for him, but also to support Musa's mission.

chapters.title: The title of nabi
chapters.content: The term nabi confirms that Harun received revelation and conveyed Allah's message. The Quran does not detail the circumstances of his prophethood here, but clearly affirms his rank. Harun thus enters the chain of honored prophets that the Book preserves for believers, alongside his brother Musa.

references.content: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥ مِن رَّحْمَتِنَآ أَخَاهُ هَـٰرُونَ نَبِيًّا
references.translation: And out of Our grace We granted him his brother Aaron as a prophet.
references.relevance: Main verse establishing Harun's prophethood as a gift of mercy.

glossary.definition: Brother of Musa, a prophet mentioned in the Quran.
Prophet who receives revelation and calls to the worship of Allah alone.
Divine mercy. Harun was granted to Musa through Allah's rahma.

harun_helper.title: Harun, assistant and support of Musa
harun_helper.introduction: When Musa received his mission, he asked Allah for a wazir (assistant) from his family: his brother Harun. He also wished for his speech to be clear so it would be understood. Musa said: "Give me a helper from my family: Aaron, my brother; augment my strength through him; let him share my task." (20:29-32) Allah granted his request: "Moses, your request is granted." (20:36)
harun_helper.conclusion: Harun was chosen by Musa himself as a prophetic companion. Allah accepted this request. Harun thus became Musa's auxiliary in conveying the message, with speech apt to be heard and understood.
harun_helper.moral_lesson: Asking for sincere help in good is prophetic wisdom. Harun illustrates the noble role of one who supports truth alongside a messenger.
harun_helper.historical_context: The Quran presents Musa's request without relating here the entire confrontation with Pharaoh. The focus is on Harun's role as wazir.

chapters.title: Musa's request
chapters.content: Musa invoked Allah to untie the knot of his tongue so that people would understand his speech (20:27-28), then asked: "Give me a helper from my family: Aaron, my brother." (20:29-30) He explained that Harun would strengthen his strength, share his mission, and that together they would glorify and remember Allah abundantly (20:31-34). Musa acknowledged that Allah always watched over them (20:35).

chapters.title: Allah grants Harun
chapters.content: Allah replied: "Moses, your request is granted." (20:36) Harun was thus designated as wazir: one who bears the burden with the prophet, shoulders the mission, and helps make the message understood. The Quran highlights Harun's clarity of speech, the quality for which Musa chose him. Harun is not a mere companion: he is a prophet associated with Musa's prophetic work.

references.content: وَٱجْعَل لِّى وَزِيرًا مِّنْ أَهْلِى ۝ هَـٰرُونَ أَخِى ۝ ٱشْدُدْ بِهِۦٓ أَزْرِى ۝ وَأَشْرِكْهُ فِىٓ أَمْرِى
references.translation: Give me a helper from my family: Aaron, my brother; augment my strength through him; let him share my task.
references.relevance: Establishes Harun as wazir and partner in Musa's mission.
Confirms that Allah accepted Musa's request concerning Harun.

glossary.definition: Assistant, minister. Harun was Musa's wazir in his mission.
Reinforcement, support. Musa asked that Harun strengthen his strength.
Affair, mission. Harun was associated with Musa's amr (mission).

harun_with_musa.title: Harun and Musa, favored and saved
harun_with_musa.introduction: Allah honors Harun alongside Musa in Surah As-Saffat: "We also bestowed Our favor on Moses and Aaron. We saved them and their people from great distress. We helped them, so they were the ones to succeed." (37:114-116) The Quran continues by mentioning the clear Scripture, the right path, lasting praise, and peace upon them both.
harun_with_musa.conclusion: Harun shares with Musa divine favors, salvation, and victory. Allah concludes: "Peace be upon Moses and Aaron! Truly they were among Our faithful servants." (37:120-122) Harun is counted among the muhsinin (doers of good) rewarded by Allah.
harun_with_musa.moral_lesson: Fidelity to Allah leads to salvation and honor. Harun, alongside Musa, shows that the prophetic mission can be carried out in brotherhood under divine protection.
harun_with_musa.historical_context: The Quran summarizes here the blessings granted to Musa and Harun without detailing each episode. The believer reflects on these Quranic honors.

chapters.title: Favors, salvation, and victory
chapters.content: Allah says: "We also bestowed Our favor on Moses and Aaron. We saved them and their people from great distress. We helped them, so they were the ones to succeed." (37:114-116) Harun is associated with Musa in mann (favor), najat (salvation) from kurb al-azim (great distress), and nasr (victory). The Quran places Harun at the same level of protection and triumph as his brother.

chapters.title: Scripture, right path, and peace
chapters.content: Allah continues: "We gave them the Scripture that makes things clear, and guided them to the right path. We let them be praised by succeeding generations. Peace be upon Moses and Aaron! Truly they were among Our faithful servants." (37:117-122) Harun thus received the kitab al-mustabin (clear Scripture) and was guided to as-sirat al-mustaqim (the right path). His name remains honored through generations, under divine salam, as a believing servant of Allah.

references.content: وَلَقَدْ مَنَنَّا عَلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ ۝ وَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُمَا وَقَوْمَهُمَا مِنَ ٱلْكَرْبِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ ۝ وَنَصَرْنَـٰهُمْ فَكَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْغَـٰلِبِينَ
references.translation: We also bestowed Our favor on Moses and Aaron. We saved them and their people from great distress. We helped them, so they were the ones to succeed.
references.relevance: Affirms the favors, salvation, and victory shared by Musa and Harun.

references.content: سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ ۝ إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ ۝ إِنَّهُمَا مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
references.translation: Peace be upon Moses and Aaron! This is how We reward those who do good. Truly they were among Our faithful servants.
references.relevance: Proclaims peace upon Harun and counts him among the believing servants.

glossary.definition: Favor, blessing. Allah granted mann to Musa and Harun.
Peace, safety. Allah sends salam upon Musa and Harun.
Doer of good. Musa and Harun are rewarded as muhsinin.

harun_furqan.title: Harun and the Furqan
harun_furqan.introduction: Allah says in Surah Al-Anbiya: "We gave Moses and Aaron [the Scripture] that distinguishes right from wrong, a light and a reminder for those who are mindful of God." (21:48) The Furqan is what separates truth from falsehood. Harun was a co-recipient with Musa, a sign of his prophetic rank in transmitting guidance.
harun_furqan.conclusion: Harun received the Furqan, light, and dhikr (reminder) intended for the muttaqin (the pious). He was not a mere companion: he shared the revelation and the responsibility of guiding toward truth.
harun_furqan.moral_lesson: Divine guidance illuminates those who fear Allah. Harun reminds us that prophethood involves carrying a clear message that separates truth from error.
harun_furqan.historical_context: The Quran associates Harun with Musa in receiving the Furqan, without developing here the detailed content of the revelation.

chapters.title: The Furqan granted to both brothers
chapters.content: Allah says: "We gave Moses and Aaron [the Scripture] that distinguishes right from wrong, a light and a reminder for those who are mindful of God." (21:48) The Furqan is the criterion that distinguishes good from evil, truth from falsehood. Harun is explicitly named alongside Musa as a recipient of this revelation. This confirms his prophetic role in calling to tawhid and piety.

chapters.title: Light and reminder for the pious
chapters.content: The verse adds wadi (light) and dhikr (reminder) lil-muttaqin (for the pious). Harun thus participated in transmitting guidance that illuminates hearts and reminds of duties toward Allah. The believer reflects on this triple gift — Furqan, light, reminder — and recognizes in Harun a prophet associated with divine teaching, not a secondary figure in Musa's story.

references.content: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ ٱلْفُرْقَانَ وَضِيَآءً وَذِكْرًا لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
references.translation: We gave Moses and Aaron [the Scripture] that distinguishes right from wrong, a light and a reminder for those who are mindful of God.
references.relevance: Central verse associating Harun with receiving the Furqan.

glossary.definition: The Criterion. What distinguishes truth from falsehood. Given to Musa and Harun.
Light. Divine guidance illuminating the hearts of the pious.
Pious, one who fears Allah and obeys Him.

lessons_from_harun.title: Lessons from the prophet Harun
lessons_from_harun.introduction: The Quran mentions Harun in precise verses: prophet granted by mercy (19:53), assistant of Musa (20:29-36), favored and saved with him (37:114-122), co-recipient of the Furqan (21:48). By reflecting on these texts, the believer discovers Harun's prophetic role without adding unverified narratives.
lessons_from_harun.conclusion: Harun remains a model of support in truth, shared prophethood, and fidelity. His story in the Quran invites honoring prophets through revelation and seeking mutual aid in good.
lessons_from_harun.moral_lesson: Adhering to authentic verses protects faith. Harun's qualities — prophethood, assistance, fidelity, guidance — inspire the believer daily.

chapters.title: Knowing Harun through the Quran
chapters.content: Allah names Harun nabi (19:53), wazir of Musa (20:29-32), associated with salvation and victory (37:114-116), recipient of the Furqan (21:48), and recipient of salam (37:120). The Muslim honors Harun through what Allah said, without inventing details. This is the sound method: knowing prophets through revelation, focusing on their role as the Quran presents it.

chapters.title: Aiming for prophetic mutual support
chapters.content: Harun teaches that the mission of truth can be carried out in brotherhood: Musa asked for his help, Allah granted it. Harun strengthened clear speech, shared the burden, and received guidance. The believer can draw inspiration from this mutual aid in good, clarity in speech, and fidelity to Allah, following the traces of ibad al-mu'minin (believing servants) whom Allah counts among the muhsinin.

references.content: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥ مِن رَّحْمَتِنَآ أَخَاهُ هَـٰرُونَ نَبِيًّا
references.translation: And out of Our grace We granted him his brother Aaron as a prophet.
references.relevance: Summary of the Quranic honors granted to Harun.

references.content: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ ٱلْفُرْقَانَ وَضِيَآءً وَذِكْرًا لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
references.translation: We gave Moses and Aaron [the Scripture] that distinguishes right from wrong, a light and a reminder for those who are mindful of God.
references.relevance: Recalls Harun's role in transmitting guidance.

glossary.definition: Oneness of Allah. Message transmitted by the prophets, including Harun.
Believing servants. Musa and Harun are among them according to the Quran.
